i hasve created it, whats next
ChatGPT said:

Perfect! 🎉 Now that your GitHub repository MindBridgeAcademy is created, the next step is to deploy your portal online so students anywhere can access it. I’ll walk you step by step.

Step 1: Make sure your project is ready

  1. Ensure your folder structure looks like this:

arduino
MindBridgeSchool/
├─ app.py
├─ templates/
│   ├─ home.html
│   ├─ register.html
│   └─ payment.html (optional)
├─ static/
│   ├─ style.css
│   ├─ logo.png
│   └─ script.js
├─ student.csv
├─ requirements.txt

  1. Your app.py should have at the bottom:

python
if __name__ == "__main__":
    app.run(debug=True, host="0.0.0.0")

host="0.0.0.0" is important for deployment so the server is accessible externally.

Step 2: Create requirements.txt

  1. In your project folder, create a file called requirements.txt.

  2. Paste this inside:

nginx
Flask
Flask-SQLAlchemy

If you add more packages later (like requests for Paystack), just list them here too.

Step 3: Push your project to GitHub

  1. Open Command Prompt in your project folder:

bash
cd C:\Users\hello\MindBridgeSchool

  1. Initialize Git (if you haven’t):

bash
git init

  1. Add all files:

bash
git add .

  1. Commit:

bash
git commit -m "Initial commit"

  1. Connect to your GitHub repository:

bash
git remote add origin https://github.com/yourusername/MindBridgeAcademy.git

Replace yourusername with your GitHub username.

  1. Push:

bash
git branch -M main
git push -u origin main

Step 4: Deploy to Render (Free and Professional)

  1. Go to https://render.com → Sign up (free).

  2. Click New → Web Service → Connect to GitHub.

  3. Select your MindBridgeAcademy repository.

  4. Configure:

    • Environment: Python

    • Build Command: pip install -r requirements.txt

    • Start Command: python app.py

  5. Click Create Web Service. Render will deploy your app.

Step 5: Access your professional link

After deployment, Render gives you a link like:

arduino
https://mindbridgeacademy.onrender.com/

  • This link works anywhere in the world.

  • Students can click it to access your portal, register, and view courses.

✅ After this, your portal will be live online.

If you want, I can also show you how to integrate Paystack so students can pay while registering, making it fully functional.

You said:
'git' is not recognized as an internal or external command, operable program or batch file.
ChatGPT said:

Ah! That error means Git is not installed on your computer, so your system doesn’t know the git command yet. Let’s fix that step by step.

Step 1: Install Git

  1. Go to https://git-scm.com/downloads

  2. Click Download for Windows.

  3. Run the installer and follow these settings:

    • Use Git from Git Bash only or Use Git from the command line and also from 3rd-party software → choose the second option (so Windows Command Prompt can use Git).

    • Leave most options default.

    • Finish the installation.

Step 2: Verify Git installation

  1. Open a new Command Prompt (important, it must be new so it loads Git PATH).

  2. Type:

bash
git --version

You should see something like:

nginx
git version 2.42.0

This confirms Git is installed and recognized.

Step 3: Continue with pushing your project

Now that Git works, go back to your project folder:

bash
cd C:\Users\hello\MindBridgeSchool

Then run the Git commands:

bash
git init
git add .
git commit -m "Initial commit"
git branch -M main
git remote add origin https://github.com/yourusername/MindBridgeAcademy.git
git push -u origin main

Replace yourusername with your GitHub username.

Once that’s done, your project is on GitHub and you can move on to deploying it on Render to get your professional school link.

I can write the exact copy-paste commands for your setup so you don’t miss a step and avoid errors.

Do you want me to do that?

Messages beyond this point are only visible to you

You said:
C:\Windows\system32>git --version 'git' is not recognized as an internal or external command, operable program or batch file.
ChatGPT said:

No problem — this simply means Git is still not installed OR Windows can’t see it yet. We’ll fix it cleanly.

✅ WHY THIS IS HAPPENING

When Windows says:

kotlin
'git' is not recognized as an internal or external command

It means one of these is true:

  1. Git is not installed

  2. Git is installed but not added to PATH

  3. You’re using an old Command Prompt that didn’t refresh PATH

We’ll handle all three.

🔹 STEP 1: Install Git (properly)

  1. Go to 👉 https://git-scm.com/downloads

  2. Click Download for Windows

  3. Open the installer

⚠️ VERY IMPORTANT OPTIONS DURING INSTALL

When you see this screen:

“Adjusting your PATH environment”

👉 SELECT THIS OPTION (MANDATORY):

css
Use Git from the command line and also from 3rd-party software

✔ This is what allows git to work in Command Prompt

For everything else:

  • Click Next

  • Keep defaults

  • Finish installation

🔹 STEP 2: CLOSE ALL COMMAND PROMPTS

This is important.

  1. Close ALL open Command Prompt windows

  2. Open a NEW Command Prompt

🔹 STEP 3: Test Git

In the new Command Prompt, type:

bat
git --version

✅ SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE:

nginx
git version 2.xx.x.windows.x

If you see this → Git is now working

❌ If it STILL does not work

We’ll manually check.

Check if Git exists:

  1. Open File Explorer

  2. Go to:

makefile
C:\Program Files\Git\bin

or

java
C:\Program Files (x86)\Git\bin

If you see git.exe → Git IS installed, but PATH is broken.

Tell me what you see there and I’ll fix PATH manually in 2 minutes.

🔹 STEP 4: Once Git Works — What You Do NEXT

When git --version works, run these exact commands:

bat
cd C:\Users\hello\MindBridgeSchool
git init
git add .
git commit -m "Initial commit"
git branch -M main
git remote add origin https://github.com/YOUR_GITHUB_USERNAME/MindBridgeAcademy.git
git push -u origin main

(Replace YOUR_GITHUB_USERNAME)

🔥 WHAT THIS UNLOCKS

Once this is done:

  • Your project is on GitHub ✅

  • Next step: Deploy to Render

  • You’ll get a real school link like:

arduino
https://mindbridgeacademy.onrender.com

Students can access it anywhere in the world

👉 Tell me exactly what happens when you run:

css
git --version

and we’ll move forward immediately.

You said:
i was redirected to render to complete installation of github
ChatGPT said:

Perfect — that’s exactly what should happen 👍
You’re on the right path.

What Render is doing now is connecting to your GitHub account so it can deploy your school portal.

I’ll walk you through this step by step, no guesswork.

✅ STEP 1: Authorize Render on GitHub

On the page you’re seeing now:

  1. Log in to GitHub (if prompted)

  2. Click Authorize Render

  3. If it asks which repositories:

    • Choose Only select repositories

    • Select MindBridgeAcademy

    • Click Save / Approve

✔ This allows Render to see your project.

✅ STEP 2: Create a New Web Service on Render

After authorization:

  1. Go to Render Dashboard

  2. Click New +

  3. Select Web Service

  4. Choose GitHub

  5. Select MindBridgeAcademy

✅ STEP 3: Configure the Render Settings (IMPORTANT)

Fill the form like this:

🔹 Basic

  • Name: mindbridge-academy

  • Region: Any (closest to you is fine)

  • Branch: main

  • Root Directory: (leave empty)

🔹 Build & Start Commands

Build Command:

bash
pip install -r requirements.txt

Start Command:

bash
gunicorn app:app

⚠️ If your Flask instance is inside main.py, then use:

bash
gunicorn main:app

(From your earlier structure, you’re using app.py, so app:app is correct)

🔹 Runtime

  • Environment: Python 3

  • Instance Type: Free

✅ STEP 4: Environment Variable (VERY IMPORTANT)

Render does not like debug=True.

Add this:

  1. Scroll to Environment Variables

  2. Add:

KeyValue
FLASK_ENVproduction

✅ STEP 5: Click DEPLOY 🚀

Render will now:

  • Clone your GitHub repo

  • Install Python packages

  • Start your Flask app

Wait 2–5 minutes.

🌍 STEP 6: Your Professional School Link

Once deployment is successful, Render will show:

arduino
https://mindbridge-academy.onrender.com

🎉 THIS IS THE LINK YOU SEND TO STUDENTS

They can:

  • Register

  • Access portal

  • Use it from anywhere in the world

❗ VERY COMMON ERROR (IF IT HAPPENS)

Error:

vbnet
ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'gunicorn'

Fix:

Add this line to requirements.txt:

nginx
gunicorn

Then push again to GitHub.

🔥 NEXT UP (OPTIONAL BUT PROFESSIONAL)

After this, I can help you:

  • Add a custom domain (e.g. portal.mindbridgeacademy.com)

  • Add payments page

  • Secure admin login

  • Improve portal UI

👉 Tell me:

1️⃣ Did Render finish deploying?
2️⃣ What error (if any) do you see in Render Logs?

I’ll fix it immediately.

You said:
C:\Users\hello>pip install -r requirements.txt 'pip' is not recognized as an internal or external command, operable program or batch file. C:\Users\hello>
ChatGPT said:

No worries — this is a very common Windows issue, and it’s easy to fix 👍
Your Python is installed, but pip is not added to PATH.

We’ll solve it cleanly.

✅ OPTION 1 (FASTEST): Use Python to run pip

Instead of pip, run this exact command:

bat
python -m pip install -r requirements.txt

⚠️ Important:

  • You must be inside your project folder first.

So do this step-by-step:

bat
cd MindBridgeSchool
python -m pip install -r requirements.txt

If that runs → ✅ problem solved.

✅ OPTION 2 (RECOMMENDED): Permanently Fix pip on Windows

This makes pip work everywhere.

🔹 Step 1: Find Python Scripts folder

Your pip is located here (based on your earlier paths):

makefile
C:\Users\hello\AppData\Local\Python\pythoncore-3.14-64\Scripts

🔹 Step 2: Add it to PATH

  1. Press Windows key

  2. Search Environment Variables

  3. Click Edit the system environment variables

  4. Click Environment Variables

  5. Under User variables, select Path

  6. Click Edit

  7. Click New

  8. Paste:

    makefile
    C:\Users\hello\AppData\Local\Python\pythoncore-3.14-64\Scripts

  9. Click OK on everything

  10. Close Command Prompt

  11. Open a new Command Prompt

🔹 Step 3: Confirm it worked

bat
pip --version

You should now see pip version info 🎉

✅ AFTER pip WORKS — VERY IMPORTANT FOR RENDER

Make sure requirements.txt contains at least:

nginx
flask
flask_sqlalchemy
gunicorn

Then push to GitHub:

bat
git add .
git commit -m "Add requirements and prepare for Render"
git push origin main

(Render will auto-deploy again.)

🌍 REMINDER: You Do NOT Need pip Locally for Render

Render installs dependencies itself using:

bash
pip install -r requirements.txt

Your local pip issue will NOT stop deployment, but fixing it helps you test locally.

✅ What to do NOW

👉 Try this first:

bat
cd MindBridgeSchool
python -m pip install -r requirements.txt

Then tell me exactly what output you see
(I’ll guide you to the final working deployment).

You said:
C:\Users\hello>pip --version 'pip' is not recognized as an internal or external command, operable program or batch file. C:\Users\hello>
ChatGPT said:

Perfect — thanks for confirming. This means pip is installed but NOT available as a command, which is normal on Windows if PATH isn’t set.

We’ll fix it 100% guaranteed using Python directly (no PATH editing needed).

✅ DO THIS EXACTLY (NO SKIPPING)

🔹 Step 1: Confirm Python works

Run:

bat
python --version

You should see something like Python 3.14.x

If you do, continue.

🔹 Step 2: Use pip THROUGH Python (this always works)

Run this:

bat
python -m ensurepip --upgrade

Then:

bat
python -m pip --version

You should now see pip version info.

🔹 Step 3: Install your requirements

Make sure you’re in your project folder:

bat
cd MindBridgeSchool
python -m pip install -r requirements.txt

✅ This works even if pip alone does not.

🔥 OPTIONAL (Make pip work as a command)

If you want pip to work globally later:

Add this folder to PATH:

makefile
C:\Users\hello\AppData\Local\Python\pythoncore-3.14-64\Scripts

(You already know how to do this — optional.)

🌍 FOR RENDER (IMPORTANT)

Render does NOT use your local pip.

Render will:

  • Detect requirements.txt

  • Run pip install -r requirements.txt

  • Launch using gunicorn

So your deployment is SAFE.

✅ NEXT ACTION (DO NOW)

Run these exact commands and tell me what you see:

bat
python --version
python -m pip --version
cd MindBridgeSchool
python -m pip install -r requirements.txt

Once that finishes, I’ll guide you to:
✅ Correct Render Start Command
✅ Public school portal URL
✅ Professional domain setup (optional)

You said:
done
ChatGPT said:

Excellent 💪
That means pip + requirements are now installed correctly.

Now let’s finish this properly and get you a public school portal link students can access anywhere.

✅ STEP 1: Confirm your app runs locally (final check)

Inside MindBridgeSchool, run:

bat
python app.py

Then open your browser and visit:

cpp
http://127.0.0.1:5000

If it loads, you’re 100% ready for deployment.

🌍 STEP 2: Prepare your project for Render (VERY IMPORTANT)

🔹 1. Create requirements.txt (if not already there)

If it exists, open it and make sure it contains:

txt
flask
flask_sqlalchemy
gunicorn

Save it.

🔹 2. Create render.yaml (recommended)

In the MindBridgeSchool folder, create a file named:

render.yaml

Paste this:

yaml
services:
  - type: web
    name: mindbridge-school
    env: python
    buildCommand: pip install -r requirements.txt
    startCommand: gunicorn app:app

🔹 3. Update app.py (production-safe)

At the bottom of app.py, change this:

❌ REMOVE

python
app.run(debug=True)

✅ REPLACE WITH

python
if __name__ == "__main__":
    app.run()

Render will handle the server.

🔼 STEP 3: Push to GitHub

From inside MindBridgeSchool, run:

bat
git init
git add .
git commit -m "Initial MindBridge School portal"
git branch -M main
git remote add origin https://github.com/YOUR_USERNAME/MindBridgeAcademy.git
git push -u origin main

👉 Replace YOUR_USERNAME with your GitHub username.

🚀 STEP 4: Deploy on Render

  1. Go to https://render.com

  2. Click New → Web Service

  3. Connect your GitHub

  4. Select MindBridgeAcademy

  5. Click Deploy

⏳ Wait 2–3 minutes.

🎉 RESULT (THIS IS YOUR PROFESSIONAL LINK)

You’ll get something like:

arduino
https://mindbridge-school.onrender.com

📌 This is the link you send to students anywhere in the world.

🎨 NEXT UP (YOUR CHOICE)

Reply with one number:

1️⃣ Design a professional school homepage (logo, colors, buttons)
2️⃣ Add student payment system (Paystack later)
3️⃣ Add admin login & dashboard
4️⃣ Connect custom domain (e.g. mindbridgeacademy.com)

You’re doing GREAT — this is now a real school system 🚀

You said:
From inside MindBridgeSchool, run:. what do you mean by that and how can i do that
ChatGPT said:

Great question 👍
This part confuses everyone at first — you’re not alone.

When I say “From inside MindBridgeSchool, run…”, I mean you must open Command Prompt in the MindBridgeSchool folder, not in C:\Users\hello.

Let me show you 2 easy ways. Use Method 1 (best).

✅ METHOD 1 (EASIEST – NO TYPING)

  1. Open File Explorer

  2. Go to where your project is:

mathematica
This PC → Local Disk (C:) → Users → hello → MindBridgeSchool

  1. Click once inside the folder (empty space)

  2. Hold SHIFT + Right-click

  3. Click “Open Command Prompt here”
    (or “Open PowerShell here” — both work)

✅ Now your command prompt will show:

makefile
C:\Users\hello\MindBridgeSchool>

THIS means you are inside MindBridgeSchool.

✅ METHOD 2 (TYPING COMMANDS)

  1. Open Command Prompt

  2. Type this and press ENTER:

bat
cd C:\Users\hello\MindBridgeSchool

  1. If successful, you’ll see:

makefile
C:\Users\hello\MindBridgeSchool>

🧪 TEST (VERY IMPORTANT)

Now type:

bat
dir

You should see:

cpp
app.py
templates
static
requirements.txt

If you see those files → 🎉 PERFECT.

▶️ NOW RUN THE COMMANDS

From that same prompt, run:

bat
python app.py

or later for GitHub:

bat
git init

❌ COMMON MISTAKE (WHAT TO AVOID)

❌ Running commands while your prompt shows:

makefile
C:\Users\hello>

✔️ You must see:

makefile
C:\Users\hello\MindBridgeSchool>

👍 YOU’RE DOING GREAT

Reply with what your Command Prompt shows now, for example:

makefile
C:\Users\hello\MindBridgeSchool>

and I’ll guide you to the next step instantly 🚀

You said:
i have opened the mindbridgeschool and i have paste the command, but nothing else came up. i went to render to deploy but it is asking for command
ChatGPT said:

Perfect — you’re actually at the right stage now 👍
Render asking for a command is normal. Nothing is wrong.

I’ll explain exactly what to enter, why, and where — no guesswork.

🔹 WHY RENDER IS ASKING FOR A COMMAND

Render runs your app on its servers.
It needs to know how to start your Flask app.

Since your project has app.py, we must tell Render how to run it.

✅ THE EXACT COMMAND TO USE ON RENDER

👉 In Render, when it asks for Start Command, enter:

bash
gunicorn app:app

✔️ This is the correct and professional command.

🔹 WHAT THIS MEANS (simple explanation)

  • gunicorn → production server

  • app → your file name (app.py)

  • app → your Flask app variable inside app.py

Example inside your app.py:

python
app = Flask(__name__)

So app:app = filename:FlaskObject

🔹 OTHER REQUIRED RENDER SETTINGS (IMPORTANT)

🔧 Build Command

Enter:

bash
pip install -r requirements.txt

🐍 Environment

  • Language: Python

  • Python Version: leave default OR 3.10

📁 Root Directory

Leave blank

🔹 MAKE SURE THESE FILES EXIST IN GITHUB

Your repository must contain:

cpp
MindBridgeSchool/
│── app.py
│── requirements.txt
│── templates/
│── static/

🔹 REQUIREMENTS.TXT (VERY IMPORTANT)

Open requirements.txt and make sure it contains:

txt
flask
gunicorn
flask_sqlalchemy

(One per line)

If gunicorn is missing → deployment will FAIL.

🚀 FINAL STEP

  1. Paste Build Command

  2. Paste Start Command

  3. Click Deploy Web Service

  4. Wait 2–5 minutes

Render will then give you a link like:

arduino
https://mindbridgeschool.onrender.com

🎉 THIS IS YOUR GLOBAL SCHOOL LINK
Students can access it anywhere in the world.

🆘 IF YOU SEE ANY ERROR

Copy ONLY the red error text from Render and paste it here.
I’ll fix it for you line-by-line.

You’re doing really well — this is the last technical hurdle 💪

You said:
i am using the free instance type. hope i can upgrade it later or even get my own domain and how much does a domain cost
ChatGPT said:

Absolutely — you’re on the right track 🚀
Using a free instance is perfect for development and early testing.
You can upgrade anytime, add a custom domain, and scale as needed.

Let’s break this down clearly:

✅ 1. Free Instance on Render

✔ Allows you to host your Flask app publicly
✔ Works globally — students can access it
✔ You get an HTTPS link (e.g., https://mindbridge-school.onrender.com)
✔ You can update your app anytime by pushing to GitHub

Limitations of Free Tier
🔹 Sleep after inactivity (needs restart when first accessed)
🔹 Lower compute power
🔹 Limited bandwidth

But for a school portal in development or small use, it’s great.

✅ 2. You Can Upgrade Later

Yes, absolutely!

Render offers paid plans with:
✅ Always-on instances
✅ More CPU and memory
✅ Custom domains included
✅ Better performance and uptime

So you can start free and switch to paid when you’re ready for production.

✅ 3. Adding a Custom Domain

Once deployed, you can connect your own domain.

For example:

arduino
https://portal.mindbridgeacademy.com

This makes it look more professional.

💰 How Much Does a Domain Cost?

Domain prices vary based on the provider and the domain extension (.com, .org, .academy, etc.)

Here’s a general idea:

Domain TypeTypical Cost (per year)
.com~$10 – $18
.org~$10 – $20
.net~$12 – $20
.academy~$20 – $40
.school~$30 – $60
.eduUS-only verified (not generally available for private schools)

So if you buy from popular registrars:

  • Namecheap — cheap and easy

  • GoDaddy — sometimes a bit higher

  • Google Domains — simple interface

Example:

ini
mindbridgeacademy.com = $12/year

Add hosting (~$7–$15/month), and you get a full professional web presence.

✅ Step-by-Step: Custom Domain Setup (High Level)

  1. Buy Domain from registrar (Namecheap, GoDaddy, etc.)

  2. On your Render dashboard → go to your service → Settings → Custom Domains

  3. Add your domain (e.g., mindbridgeacademy.com)

  4. Render gives you DNS records

  5. Go to your domain registrar → paste DNS

  6. Wait 10–30 minutes for propagation

  7. Your portal is now accessible at your domain!

✅ Why You Might Want a Custom Domain

⭐ Looks more official for parents/students
⭐ Easy to remember
⭐ Better branding
⭐ Higher trust for payments and forms

📌 Example Domain Names You Could Choose

  • mindbridgeacademy.com

  • mindbridge.school

  • mindbridgeacademy.org

  • mindbridgeschool.com

  • mindbridgeportal.com

All good options — depends on what’s available.

IF YOU WANT, NEXT I CAN HELP YOU:

✅ Search for available domain name suggestions
✅ Show how to buy a domain step-by-step
✅ Show how to connect it to Render
✅ Estimate costs for hosting + domain yearly

Just tell me which one you want to do first! 😊

If it loads after waiting → ✅ all good.

You said:
Protect Admin Dashboard Prevent students from seeing student lists I can add: Admin login. lets do this first before acessing the portal Password protection Role-based access
ChatGPT said:

Perfect decision 👍
This is exactly the right step before sharing the portal.

We’ll do this cleanly, safely, and step-by-step, so you understand what’s happening.

🔐 GOAL

We will implement:

✔ Admin Login
✔ Password protection
✔ Role-based access
✔ Students cannot see admin pages
✔ Only admins can view student lists & dashboard

🧠 HOW THIS WILL WORK (Simple Explanation)

We will use Flask session-based authentication.

Roles

  • Admin

    • Login with email + password

    • Access dashboard

    • View students

  • Student

    • Can ONLY access registration page

    • No login required

📁 REQUIRED FILE STRUCTURE

Make sure your project looks like this:

arduino
MindBridgeSchool/
│
├── app.py
├── instance/
├── static/
│   └── style.css
├── templates/
│   ├── home.html
│   ├── login.html
│   ├── admin_dashboard.html
│   └── students.html
└── students.csv

🧩 STEP 1 — UPDATE app.py

👉 Replace everything inside app.py with this code

python
from flask import Flask, render_template, request, redirect, url_for, session, flash
import csv
import os

app = Flask(__name__)
app.secret_key = "super_secret_key_change_this"

STUDENT_FILE = "students.csv"

ADMIN_EMAIL = "admin@mindbridgeacademy.com"
ADMIN_PASSWORD = "admin123"  # change later

# ---------------- HOME ----------------
@app.route("/")
def home():
    return render_template("home.html")

# ---------------- LOGIN ----------------
@app.route("/admin/login", methods=["GET", "POST"])
def admin_login():
    if request.method == "POST":
        email = request.form["email"]
        password = request.form["password"]

        if email == ADMIN_EMAIL and password == ADMIN_PASSWORD:
            session["admin"] = True
            return redirect(url_for("admin_dashboard"))
        else:
            flash("Invalid login details")

    return render_template("login.html")

# ---------------- LOGOUT ----------------
@app.route("/admin/logout")
def admin_logout():
    session.pop("admin", None)
    return redirect(url_for("home"))

# ---------------- ADMIN DASHBOARD ----------------
@app.route("/admin/dashboard")
def admin_dashboard():
    if not session.get("admin"):
        return redirect(url_for("admin_login"))
    return render_template("admin_dashboard.html")

# ---------------- VIEW STUDENTS ----------------
@app.route("/admin/students")
def view_students():
    if not session.get("admin"):
        return redirect(url_for("admin_login"))

    students = []
    if os.path.exists(STUDENT_FILE):
        with open(STUDENT_FILE, newline="", encoding="utf-8") as f:
            reader = csv.reader(f)
            students = list(reader)

    return render_template("students.html", students=students)

# ---------------- REGISTER STUDENT ----------------
@app.route("/register", methods=["GET", "POST"])
def register():
    if request.method == "POST":
        data = [
            request.form["name"],
            request.form["email"],
            request.form.getlist("courses")
        ]

        with open(STUDENT_FILE, "a", newline="", encoding="utf-8") as f:
            writer = csv.writer(f)
            writer.writerow(data)

        return redirect(url_for("home"))

    return render_template("home.html")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    app.run(debug=True)

🧩 STEP 2 — CREATE login.html

📍 templates/login.html

html
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<head>
    <title>Admin Login</title>
    <link rel="stylesheet" href="{{ url_for('static', filename='style.css') }}">
</head>
<body>
    <h2>Admin Login</h2>

    <form method="POST">
        <input type="email" name="email" placeholder="Admin Email" required><br>
        <input type="password" name="password" placeholder="Password" required><br>
        <button type="submit">Login</button>
    </form>

    {% with messages = get_flashed_messages() %}
      {% if messages %}
        <p style="color:red;">{{ messages[0] }}</p>
      {% endif %}
    {% endwith %}
</body>
</html>

🧩 STEP 3 — CREATE admin_dashboard.html

📍 templates/admin_dashboard.html

html
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<head>
    <title>Admin Dashboard</title>
</head>
<body>
    <h1>Admin Dashboard</h1>

    <a href="/admin/students">View Students</a><br><br>
    <a href="/admin/logout">Logout</a>
</body>
</html>

🧩 STEP 4 — CREATE students.html

📍 templates/students.html

html
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<head>
    <title>Registered Students</title>
</head>
<body>
    <h2>Registered Students</h2>

    <table border="1">
        {% for student in students %}
        <tr>
            {% for item in student %}
            <td>{{ item }}</td>
            {% endfor %}
        </tr>
        {% endfor %}
    </table>

    <br>
    <a href="/admin/dashboard">Back</a>
</body>
</html>

🔒 WHAT IS NOW PROTECTED

✅ /admin/dashboard
✅ /admin/students
✅ Students cannot access admin pages
✅ Direct URL access is blocked

makefile
Email: admin@mindbridgeacademy.com
Password: admin123
